posts.text: 9 Trump's dilemma is whether he can accept ending this war having only degraded Iran's capabilities--leaving the country in the hands of a Khamenei whose worldview is just as dogmatic as the man he killed, and three decades younger. theatlantic.com/internationa…

8 For the US the war--less than two weeks in--thus far appears a military success but a political failure. The campaign has effectively degraded the regime's malign capacities, but as of now it has seemingly strengthened the regime’s cohesion and not changed its character.

7 Mojtaba Khamenei was reportedly named after one of Khamenei's self-proclaimed heroes, the 20th century Islamist radical Mojtaba Navab Safavi, who orchestrated the murders of some of Iran's top political and thought leaders before he himself was executed.

6 Siamak Namazi, a US citizen held hostage for eight years by the IRGC, described Taeb as “the most hard-line of the hard-line” and “one of the most evil figures in the Islamic Republic.” Namazi believes that Taeb, with Mojtaba’s blessing, has been behind the taking of hostages.

5 Mojtaba’s inner circle includes Hossein Taeb, the former head of IRGC intelligence; Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, a former IRGC commander and the current speaker of Parliament; and Hossein Fadaei, one of Ali Khamenei’s chief enforcers. All are veteran practitioners of repression.

4 A source described Mojtaba as “more radical” but “much less capable” than his father, a poor speaker with the stunted interpersonal skills of a dictator’s son. “When people hear his maiden speech as leader...they will see his lack of presence.” nitter.net/maziarbahari/status/20…


Maziar Bahari (@maziarbahari)

Khamenei 2.0 has been an AI-generated leader so far. He has not given a single public speech and has not met anyone in person on camera. But this documentary was released within hours o

3 Mojtaba begins with a huge popularity and legitimacy deficit: Ayatollah Khomeini, the leader of the 1979 revolution that deposed a hereditary monarchy to establish a theocracy, called hereditary succession a “sinister, evil system of government” that “has no place in Islam."

2 Reportedly injured and in hiding (eluding assassination), Mojtaba will attempt to lead a bankrupt regime that is simultaneously fighting full-blown wars against the United States and Israel and against much of its own population.

1 Instead of producing an Iran's Delcy Rodriguez, the war has for now produced a budding Iranian Kim Jong Un. A profile of Mojtaba Khamenei, including from a Tehran source who's long known him. "They’ve just killed his family; He’s bloodthirsty now." theatlantic.com/internationa…

As expected, Mojtaba Khamenei has been named the Islamic Republc of Iran’s new Supreme Leader, succeeding his father. This is what the father of the 1979 revolution, Ayatollah Khomeini, had to say about hereditary succession.






Karim Sadjadpour (@ksadjadpour)

Mojtaba Khamenei, the hardline 56-year-old son of Iran’s late dictator and a close ally of the Revolutionary Guards, has reportedly been named Supreme Leader. He was recently injured and will likely continue to be a target of Israeli a

Iran is trying to restrain Trump by shaping American public opinion against the war.
He is now the sixth US president since 1979 consumed by events tied to Iran. @washingtonweek piped.video/qI-mJMWmpgs?si=mt8-…

Video
